mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a robust economic expansion and strong consumer demand, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between 3-month changes in PAYEMS and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically greater than zero over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation accelerates past a critical threshold, it signals that the central bank will be forced to tighten monetary policy. The resulting increase in discount rates negatively impacts equity valuations.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in both retail sales and housing starts signals a broad-based contraction in consumer and investment demand, leading to downward revisions in corporate earnings forecasts and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS < 0 AND 3-month change in HOUST < 0
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns with an accuracy greater than a random walk (50%) over a 10-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a restrictive monetary policy regime, a significant increase in the unemployment rate signals that policy has overtightened, triggering recession fears and equity market sell-offs as investors price in a sharp economic downturn.
failure_condition: The mechanism shows no predictive power (Rank IC close to zero) during historical periods where FEDFUNDS was above 2.0% and UNRATE was rising.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production reflects a healthy manufacturing and supply side of the economy. This translates to strong corporate revenues and supports higher equity prices over the short term.
failure_condition: The 6-month momentum of INDPRO is not positively correlated with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate is near the zero lower bound, it indicates an extremely accommodative monetary policy stance, which encourages risk-taking and boosts equity markets through low borrowing costs and portfolio rebalancing effects.
failure_condition: During periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, the average 60-day forward return for the S&P 500 is not statistically different from periods where FEDFUNDS > 0.5% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A positive quarter-over-quarter change in real GDP confirms broad economic strength, leading to immediate positive revisions in earnings expectations and very short-term positive equity returns as the data is absorbed by markets.
failure_condition: The sign of the quarter-over-quarter change in GDPC1 does not predict the sign of the 5-day forward S&P 500 return with an accuracy significantly above 50%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a low unemployment rate and accelerating inflation signals an overheating economy (a 'hot' Phillips Curve), forcing the central bank to pursue aggressive monetary tightening, which negatively impacts equity valuations.
failure_condition: In historical periods matching the trigger condition, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not negative or is not statistically different from zero.
